mechanism: All Deployment Stacks use deleteAll lifecycle. Any Azure resource not declared in the Bicep template is deleted on the next deployment — the template is the source of truth.
why: Without deleteAll, manually created or orphaned resources persist with no code representation — the live environment drifts from Bicep and decommissioning requires manual auditing.

related.note: deleteAll in test verifies code produces the expected Azure state before promotion to prod.
Single resource group is the stack's scope — deleteAll on it is a complete teardown.
deleteAll guarantees decommissioning leaves no residual state.
A subscription after deleteAll is guaranteed empty — makes subscription reuse safe.

violations: Deployment Stack configured with detachAll — removed resources persist with no cleanup path.
Resource created directly in Azure outside the Deployment Stack — deleted on next deployment.
Application resource inside landing-zone-resources — treated as unmanaged and deleted on next deployment.
